notes: Introduced by Sun; application portability; object encapsulation enabling data interoperability; central to Internet Infrastructure debate; JVM on all IBM platforms.
Provides standard computing services and data mappings across environments; enables Java app interoperability across different computing environments; lingua franca candidate.
Viable core for Internet Infrastructure alongside COM+; immature in 1998; supplier alliance forming around it; major vendors rushing in with proprietary EJB environments.
Microsoft's competing model for Extranet/enterprise computing; NT-centric; implemented in NT OS; COM+ on non-MS Unix platforms a key watch area; COM+ demanded new programming approach.
Microsoft's Internet Infrastructure strategy; tied to NT OS; lacks scalability and platform independence for large-scale systems; not made for inter-enterprise computing.
Aberdeen predicts all proprietary directories headed for extinction as LDAP becomes entrenched; Internet directory standard; core of public infrastructure; security and management backbone.
NT 5.0 (Windows 2000) threatening Novell's position; base for COM+/DNA strategy; dominant developer platform.
Four-component framework: ADE + ARE + Service Middleware + ISP Services; immature and rapidly evolving in 1998; Aberdeen's 'Internet Object Computing' (IOC) framework.
Internal Internet-protocol networks; already significant enterprise computing component by 1998.
Core Internet protocol; extended via MIME to transport distributed objects; web server foundation.
Internet mail standard; e-mail cited as underrated infrastructure; Aberdeen emphasizes e-mail appeal over push/publish-subscribe.
Internet directory service; Novell NetWare DNS cited; foundational ISP service.
Borland's Java compiler/IDE; licensed by Oracle for Java development; well-regarded by developers.
Long-dominant NOS; IntranetWare version incorporating Internet/extranet connectivity; threatened by NT 5.0.
Core database in Oracle's NCA; central to Oracle's Internet strategy; enterprise Java and web transaction support.
Visual Studio and Active Server Pages (ASP) enabling developers to build Internet applications using COM.
Consumers rejecting push and publish/subscribe; Aberdeen says suppliers overinvested in these vs. e-mail; limited end-user adoption.
Aberdeen's coined framework for Java+CORBA-based distributed enterprise Internet computing; ADE + ARE + SMC + ISP services.
